extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won a straightforward sprint after UAE's pace-making for Gaviria backfired, dropping their own sprinter with 25km remaining.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma's 70kph drilling for Groenewegen at 25km out paradoxically distanced rival sprinter Gaviria, weaponizing leadout work.
Teams now delaying leadout trains until sub-5km to preserve final pilot riders, fundamentally changing sprint positioning dynamics.
De Gendt counter-moved during GC team bridging attempt at 44km, exploiting predictable peloton response to inject breakaway aggression.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Dropped at 25km on pace increase meant for control, revealing poor form or positioning—UAE's leadout work counterproductive.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Positioned perfectly seventh with 1km remaining, then jumped simultaneously with Groenewegen to win easily, showing tactical maturity.
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: neutral: Taking intermediate sprint bonuses as climber signals GC ambitions beyond just mountain stages.
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: neutral: Initiated counter-attack during peloton's bridge attempt at 44km, standard opportunistic aggression yielding no stage result.
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: positive: Retained red jersey by two seconds, bonus seconds strategy from Stage 2 breakaway proving decisive.
